items.q: Prescriptive grammar describes real-world usage without judging it.
Which linguistic term matches this definition: Repetition at the end of successive clauses?
Which linguistic term matches this definition: A morpheme that cannot stand alone?
items.explanation: The matching term is bound morpheme.

items.a: A verb mood used for hypotheticals, demands, or wishes
items.explanation: Use the linguistic definition of subjunctive mood.

items.a: Two letters that represent one sound
options: Conventional spelling system of a language
Change in formality based on audience or purpose
Rules about how language should be used
Two letters that represent one sound
items.explanation: digraph means Two letters that represent one sound.

items.a: Repetition of a word or phrase at the start of clauses
items.explanation: Use the linguistic definition of anaphora.

items.q: Which linguistic term matches this definition: Description of how language is actually used?
items.explanation: The matching term is descriptive grammar.

items.a: A morpheme that can stand alone as a word
items.explanation: Use the linguistic definition of free morpheme.

items.q: Etymology studies word origins and change over time.

items.a: Rules about how language should be used
items.explanation: Use the linguistic definition of prescriptive grammar.
